danger_signals.visual_form: Heavily armed thugs with distinct visual designs; washed-out faces on sprite enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat requiring player engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Venom's symbiote children visual variants; five distinct symbiote enemies at Life Foundation Headquarters
danger_signals.meaning: Elevated threat level; enemies with enhanced abilities beyond standard thugs

danger_signals.visual_form: Heavily armed military-style enemies distinct from common thugs
danger_signals.meaning: Organized opposition faction with coordinated threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Final boss antagonist; evil symbiote visual differentiation from player symbiote
danger_signals.meaning: Critical threat state; primary antagonist requiring defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Confined underground setting; Life Foundation Headquarters location
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; trap-like setting suggesting political/corporate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Captain America, Ghost Rider, Hawkeye, Daredevil visual appearance in-game
safety_signals.meaning: Allied support arriving; momentary safety window or combat advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Ability to swap between Spider-Man and Venom; both playable sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and tactical flexibility; neither character in isolation equals weakness

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red and blue costume; distinct sprite from Venom
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears during conflict resolution moments; team-up scenarios

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Venom symbiote visual identity; large-framed aggressive sprite design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Symbiote offspring; five symbiote children created from seeds
Heavily armed thug archetype; distinct from common street-level enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: San Francisco locations; Life Foundation infrastructure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Organizational headquarters as final stage setting
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Life Foundation Headquarters; underground facilities; digger encounter locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Evil symbiote visual distinction; antagonist sprite design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Carnage symbiote (corrupted/hostile variant of Venom symbiote family)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final confrontation location; throughout level progression as narrative threat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Threat escalation - from street crime to corporate conspiracy
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy progression from generic thugs → Jury armed operatives → symbiote variants → Carnage boss; location progression from San Francisco streets → underground city → Life Foundation Headquarters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types increase in visual distinctiveness and implied combat difficulty; player forced to confront organized faction rather than random crime

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five symbiote seeds create five new symbiotes; multiple distinct symbiote enemy variants visible in final stages; visual corruption/transformation of enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy diversity increases; symbiote children appear as unique combat encounters; final symbiote confrontations at headquarters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Both characters playable together; ability to swap between contrasting visual designs (red/blue vs. black symbiote)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game requires both characters for progression; neither alone sufficient for threat level; co-op gameplay directly reflects narrative alliance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Life Foundation Headquarters as fortified location; underground city as hidden facility; confined visual spaces suggesting institutional control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players forced through organized facility; environmental structure reflects institutional power; escape sequence gameplay mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cameo appearances of Captain America, Ghost Rider, Hawkeye, Daredevil as visual allies in stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Allied characters provide assistance during specific encounters; world responds to threat level by mobilizing support cast

environmental_storytelling.example: San Francisco streets → digger encounter → underground city discovery → Life Foundation Headquarters; each environment visually communicates escalation from local crime to institutional conspiracy

Generic thugs establish street-level threat; Jury's military styling indicates organized opposition; symbiote variants signal supernatural/biotech danger; Carnage boss represents corruption of Venom's own nature

environmental_storytelling.technique: Dual protagonist design as thematic storytelling
environmental_storytelling.example: Spider-Man's contrasting visual design (red/blue) against Venom's black symbiote directly communicates moral/alliance duality; teamwork requirement reflects narrative separation anxiety theme

environmental_storytelling.technique: Facility architecture as institutional power display
environmental_storytelling.example: Life Foundation Headquarters' enclosed, fortress-like design visually communicates corporate control; underground city structures suggest hidden conspiracy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage player on contact; repetitive attack patterns; visual sameness communicates low individual threat
Armed formation suggests coordinated tactics; distinct visual appearance indicates superior training; appear as faction forces rather than individual threats
Five distinct symbiote variants suggesting individual personalities/capabilities; encounter progression indicates increasing challenge; visual distinctiveness from parent Venom suggests autonomy
Boss-level encounter; visual and mechanical distinction from all other enemies; positioned as evil counterpoint to player-controlled Venom; represents corruption/separation anxiety resolution point
Playable character swap mechanic; visual contrast to Venom indicates tactical diversity; cameo support also appears (suggests world acknowledges Spider-Man beyond controllable moments)

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Superhero cameos (Captain America, Ghost Rider, Hawkeye, Daredevil)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appear as assistance during specific encounters; visual appearance without full player control suggests world reacts to threat level; brief involvement indicates secondary support role

notes: Source provides minimal visual/mechanical detail. Game emphasizes 1990s beat-'em-up visual language: sprite-based characters, washed-out enemy faces (per GamePro review), dated visual styling. Visual communication of world-state relies heavily on traditional beat-'em-up progression: street → industrial → fortress → boss. Symbiote family theme (Venom, Carnage, five offspring) creates visual taxonomy of threat. Dual-protagonist mechanic (Spider-Man vs. Venom) is primary world-state signal—reflects narrative separation anxiety through gameplay duality. Life Foundation narrative arc visualized through location progression and enemy faction escalation. Limited documentation on specific color-coding or environmental markers; T3 tier assessment based on narrative structure inference.
